decision: Workflow schedule.cron is 0 1 * * * (01:00 UTC = 08:00 UTC+7) plus workflow_dispatch, with a UTC comment
reasoning: Actions cron is UTC (V2-002 memory lesson); user asked for 08:00 UTC+7 and a manual trigger as trivial bonus

decision: Install osspulse from checked-out source, not pip install osspulse
reasoning: Package is not published to PyPI; the V2-002 generator template's pip install osspulse would fail on CI

decision: Persist only .osspulse/state.json via git add -f + commit [skip ci] + push GITHUB_TOKEN
reasoning: Runner is stateless; state file is gitignored so must force-add; scope names exactly this file
rejected: remote object storage (both deferred per PROJECT_SPEC V3)

decision: Unchanged state must no-op (no empty commit, no failed job)
reasoning: A no-new-items run leaves state.json identical; git commit would error 'nothing to commit' and redden the job

decision: Job declares permissions: contents: write only; persistence push must not loop
reasoning: Least privilege (STRIDE E); [skip ci] + GITHUB_TOKEN-push-non-triggering prevents infinite workflow loop (STRIDE D)

decision: concurrency group with cancel-in-progress:false serializes overlapping runs
reasoning: V2-002 fcntl lock does not span separate runners; two runners racing committed state.json needs a CI-level guard
rejected: rely on fcntl lock (does not cross runners)

decision: Zero src/ changes — pure infra/config CR
reasoning: User scope item 3 explicitly forbids src/ changes; QA verifies via git diff

decision: Document LLM_API_KEY + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL as required repo Secrets; GITHUB_TOKEN auto-provided
reasoning: Operator setup-once step; matches .env.example secret names

decision: Committed CI config.toml holds no secret; secrets resolved from Actions env at run time
reasoning: config.toml uses api_key_env NAME not value; STRIDE I information-disclosure guard
rejected: commit a config with inline keys

decision: Missing/invalid committed config aborts run before persistence step
reasoning: config.toml is gitignored; without a committed CI config osspulse run raises ConfigError; persist step must be gated on run success

decision: State persistence = inline git commit-back with git add -f (force-add gitignored path), a git diff --cached --quiet clean-tree guard, commit [skip ci] + push GITHUB_TOKEN, gated on run success; permissions contents:write only; concurrency group osspulse-digest cancel-in-progress:false
reasoning: Runner stateless + state.json gitignored + clean-tree commit errors; single reasonable approach (scope=tiny). Two loop guards: [skip ci] AND GITHUB_TOKEN-push non-triggering. Least privilege. Concurrency serializes overlap since fcntl lock is per-machine
rejected: reuse V2-002 generator template (pip install osspulse + no persistence + no write perm)

decision: Used user-specified exact workflow YAML verbatim: name=OSS Pulse Daily Digest, bot=OSS Pulse Bot, persist via inline || && chain
reasoning: User provided an exact spec to implement; deviations from design skeleton are cosmetic (name, bot email) or functionally equivalent (||/&& vs if/else, no setup-python step)

decision: persist step uses if:always() — runs regardless of digest exit code
reasoning: User spec specified if:always(); a failed run with no state.json produces a clean no-op (git add -f on absent file → nothing staged → diff clean → no commit)
rejected: if:success() — would skip persist on digest failure, safe but different from user spec

decision: config.toml.ci.example created as operator template; actual config.toml commit is manual operator step
reasoning: config.toml is gitignored; operator must git add -f; example provides the safe template with no inline secrets; documented in README
rejected: committing a real config.toml in this CR (would require force-add now, operator may have different watchlist)

decision: osspulse run (no --config flag) instead of osspulse run --config config.toml
reasoning: User spec did not include --config; osspulse discovers config.toml from cwd (repo root) by convention; functionally equivalent on CI
rejected: osspulse run --config config.toml from design skeleton

decision: Spurious git push on clean-tree run — bash || and && are left-to-right: (A||B)&&C, not A||(B&&C); git push runs even when diff is quiet (no changes staged)
reasoning: S4 code bug in persist step shell script. No empty commit, no red job (push exits 0 on up-to-date repo), so AC-004 literal contract met. Low severity: cosmetic wasted API call per no-change run. Fix: wrap commit+push in subshell (git commit && git push) to enforce intended A||(B&&C) semantics
